pr.title: Add another possible shell uname for checking Windows
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Updated the root Makefile's Windows environment detection to recognize MSYS in addition to MINGW. The PR body says the existing name check was not sufficient, while checking MSYS correctly caught the author's terminal and set the WINDOWS flag.
what_changed: Added a Makefile conditional that checks whether `$(shell uname)` contains `MSYS`.
When `MSYS` is found in the shell uname output, the Makefile now sets `WINDOWS := 1`.
Left the existing `MINGW` uname check intact, making MSYS an additional accepted Windows-like shell environment.
smart_moves: Fixed the platform check at the build-system level rather than adding per-command workarounds elsewhere.
Used the same existing Makefile idiom, `ifneq ($(findstring ...,$(shell uname)),)`, keeping the patch small and consistent with the previous MINGW detection.
Broadened Windows detection without changing behavior for non-MSYS and non-MINGW systems.
decomp_lessons: For cross-platform decomp build scripts, Windows detection should account for multiple Unix-like shell environments such as MINGW and MSYS.
Small Makefile portability fixes can unblock contributors whose terminals report a different `uname` string even when they need the same Windows-specific build behavior.
When an existing environment probe is too narrow, prefer extending the recognized cases using the established local pattern.
naming_conventions: The Makefile uses a `WINDOWS` variable as a boolean flag, set to `1` when a Windows-compatible shell environment is detected.
Environment matching is based on uppercase substrings from `uname`, specifically `MINGW` and now `MSYS`.
follow_up_queries: Find other Makefile platform checks that only handle MINGW and may need MSYS or CYGWIN coverage.
Search for uses of the `WINDOWS` Makefile variable to understand which build behavior this detection controls.
Audit contributor setup documentation for required shell environments on Windows.
